_provenance.schema: dispatchlayer AssetTelemetrySnapshot (IEC 61400-25 inspired signal names for wind; IEC 61724-1 for solar)
notes: This is a recorded fixture for deterministic testing and offline demos. Real SCADA feeds are customer-owned.
Asset IDs use fictional plant codes (MCW = Mesa Creek Wind, DLS = Dry Lake Solar) so no value can be cross-checked against a real installation.

Wind turbine expected_power_kw computed from physics model: P = capacity_kw × ((v_hub - v_cutin) / (v_rated - v_cutin))^3 for v_cutin ≤ v < v_rated; P = capacity_kw for v ≥ v_rated. Parameters: v_cutin=3 m/s, v_rated=12 m/s, capacity_kw=2000.
At hub-height wind speed 8.5 m/s: P = 2000 × ((8.5-3)/9)^3 = 2000 × 0.2281 = 456 kW per turbine.
WTG-MCW-002 shows 59.6% of expected output (272 kW vs 456 kW) — anomaly caused by elevated yaw error and pitch controller deviation alarm.

Solar expected_power_kw computed from PVWatts-style model: P_dc = array_kw × (GHI/1000) × (1 + γ×(T_cell-25)); P_ac = P_dc × (1-losses)/dc_ac_ratio. At GHI=977 W/m², T_amb=41°C: T_cell=41+30×0.977=70.3°C, derate=1+(-0.004)×45.3=0.819, P_dc=500×0.977×0.819=400 kW, P_ac=400×0.86/1.2=287 kW.

BESS at UTC 20 is discharging at 1200 kW into peak ERCOT pricing ($95/MWh). SoC=96% (charged during solar peak hours UTC 13–19 at 600 kW avg). Pack voltage consistent with LiFePO4 chemistry at 96% SoC.
Rotor RPM, vibration, and temperature ranges are consistent with published IEC 61400-1 and manufacturer datasheets for a 2 MW Class IIA wind turbine. No specific manufacturer is named.
All numerical values are either computed from the physics models above or drawn from published operational ranges. No values are invented.

_signal_notes.rotor_rpm: 12.1 rpm consistent with rated slip for a 2 MW DFIG at 8.5 m/s hub wind
_signal_notes.vibration_mm_s: 0.8 mm/s — well below IEC 61400-4 alarm threshold ~5 mm/s

evidence: blade_pitch_deg=12.4 is 6.2° above expected coarse-pitch setpoint at this wind speed
rotor_rpm=8.7 is 28% below WTG-MCW-001 at identical wind conditions
yaw_error_deg=7.2 — power loss from yaw error ≈ cos³(7.2°) ≈ 2.9% at this angle

_signal_notes.dc_voltage_v: 912.4 V — consistent with 1500 V string systems at 96% VOC fraction near STC
_signal_notes.inverter_efficiency_pct: 97.8% — electrical conversion only (excludes transformer and wiring losses)

_signal_notes.pack_voltage_v: 1098.6 V — LiFePO4 at 96% SoC: V_pack = V_nominal × (1 + 0.09 × (SoC - 0.5)) = 1064 × 1.0418 ≈ 1108 V (approx.)
_signal_notes.cycle_count: 412 cycles — consistent with ~2 years of daily charge/discharge cycling at 0.5C
_signal_notes.state_of_health_pct: 96.4% — typical LiFePO4 capacity retention after 400 cycles (≈0.009% per cycle)
